_about: Workflow & automation (Automate, PowerAutomate, EnterpriseFlows, Task, TaskApproval, TaskRoleRequest, ApprovalItem, MeshCenter, MeshProcessor).

PUT /api/v1/approvalitem/batchupdate.description: Processes a batch approval action against multiple approval items in one call. Returns `{ count, results }` where `count` is the number of items and `results` is a per-item outcome list.

GET /api/v1/enterpriseFlows.description: Returns paged Enterprise Flows filtered by keyword and ordered by the requested field/direction. Requires the Workflow.WorkflowBuilder RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/enterpriseFlows.description: Creates an Enterprise Flow from a template, name, and description. Returns 201 Created with the new flow id and a `Location` header pointing at the flow. Requires the Workflow.WorkflowBuilder RACI claim.

GET /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/{id}.description: Returns Enterprise Flow details. If an activated flow is missing a trigger URL, the server publishes an error notification and logs the condition (the flow is enabled but not ready). Returns 404 when the flow cannot be found. Requires the Workflow.WorkflowBuilder RACI claim.

PUT /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/{id}.description: Updates an Enterprise Flow's status (typically to activate or deactivate) and returns the updated flow model. Requires the Workflow.WorkflowBuilder RACI claim.

DELETE /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/{id}.description: Deletes an Enterprise Flow by id and returns deletion status/details. Returns 404 when the flow cannot be found. Requires the Workflow.WorkflowBuilder RACI claim.

GET /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/templates.description: Returns the Enterprise Flow templates the organisation can choose from when creating a new flow. Requires the Workflow.WorkflowBuilder RACI claim.

POST /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/callback.description: Callback endpoint used by the Enterprise Flow execution infrastructure to deliver execution results back to CluedIn. Requires the dedicated `EnterpriseFlowsCallback` role.

GET /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/getWidgetTok.description: Returns the token used by the embedded Power Automate widget when surfacing flows in the CluedIn UI.

POST /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/webhooks/{t.description: Registers a Power Automate webhook against the supplied template id.

DELETE /api/v1/enterpriseFlows/webhooks.description: Removes registered Power Automate webhooks for the caller's organisation.

GET /api/v1/meshcenter.description: Returns paged GDPR mesh commands stored for the organisation, filtered by `isQueuedFilter` (defaults to queued only) and optionally narrowed to a single provider definition. Supports `skip`, `take`, and `ascending` sort. Requires the Preparation.MeshCenter RACI claim.

parameters.skip: Number of items to skip. Defaults to 0.
parameters.ascending: When true, sort ascending by creation date. Defaults to false.
parameters.isQueuedFilter: Filter by queued state. Defaults to true.

GET /api/MeshProcessor/AcceptsUpdate.summary: Check whether entity accepts mesh updates
GET /api/MeshProcessor/AcceptsUpdate.description: Returns whether the supplied entity can accept mesh commands (writes) given the caller's allowed provider definitions. Returns 400 if the entity cannot be found.

GET /api/MeshProcessor/AcceptVocabKey/{i.summary: Check whether vocab key accepts mesh updates
GET /api/MeshProcessor/AcceptVocabKey/{i.description: Returns whether a specific vocabulary key on the supplied entity can accept mesh commands given the caller's allowed provider definitions.

GET /api/v1/tasks.description: Returns paged user tasks assigned to the caller. Supports filtering by task type, active state, and search text; sortable by any supported field.

parameters.filterActive: Filter by active state. Defaults to true.

GET /api/v1/tasks/requests.summary: List tasks requested by current user
GET /api/v1/tasks/requests.description: Returns paged tasks that the calling user created (as the requester) rather than is assigned to (as the recipient). Same filters as `GET /api/v1/tasks`.

GET /api/v1/tasks/byobject/{objectid}/{o.description: Returns paged tasks attached to a specific object (for example rules, streams, glossary terms).

GET /api/v1/tasks/{taskId}.description: Returns the full details for a user task.

GET /api/v1/tasks/approval/{taskId}.description: Returns the details of an approval task.

PUT /api/v1/tasks/approval/approve/{task.description: Approves an approval task, queuing the original (RACI-approved) request for processing. Returns 409 Conflict if the task is already complete.

PUT /api/v1/tasks/approval/reject/{taskI.description: Rejects an approval task with a reason from the supplied model. Returns 409 Conflict if the task is already complete.

POST /api/v1/tasks/rolerequest.description: Submits a request to grant the current user a RACI claim at a given level. Returns 202 Accepted on success.

GET /api/v1/tasks/rolerequest/{taskId}.description: Returns the details of a role request task.

PUT /api/v1/tasks/rolerequest/approve/{t.description: Approves a role request and grants the requested claim. Returns 409 Conflict if the task is already complete.

PUT /api/v1/tasks/rolerequest/reject/{ta.description: Rejects a role request. Returns 409 Conflict if the task is already complete.

POST /api/deduplication/projects/{projec.description: Triggers full automation of a deduplication project (generates and commits results). Returns 400 when the project has no rules or is in a state that does not allow automation, 404 when the project cannot be found. Requires the Management.DeduplicationManagement RACI claim.

Cancels an in-progress automation. Returns 400 when the project is in a state that does not allow cancellation, 404 when the project cannot be found. Requires the Management.DeduplicationManagement RACI claim.

GET /api/deduplication/projects/{project.summary: Check whether deduplication project can be automated
GET /api/deduplication/projects/{project.description: Returns whether the project meets the preconditions for automation. Returns 404 when the project cannot be found. Requires the Management.DeduplicationManagement RACI claim.

Returns the start time of the most recent automation job for the project, as `{ LastJobStartTime }`. Returns 404 when the project cannot be found. Requires the Management.DeduplicationManagement RACI claim.
